current_implemented_build: V60.3.6 TrustMap Layer 1 Asset Rendering Correction
patch_type: TrustMap existing-bubble artwork correction over V60.3.5 overlay misfire
release_chain_status: V60.3.6 is the current implemented build. It removes the mistaken V60.3.5 floating overlay from the loader and replaces it with a narrow renderer that preserves the existing TrustMap radar, constellation layer, three-ring spatial system, Layer 1 positions, and interaction behavior while changing only the artwork inside existing Layer 1 asset bubbles. GitHub Pages browser QA is required after deploy.
top_level_tab_rule: Do not add more top-level tabs. Upgrade existing workspaces only.
public_naming_rule: Public product name is CyberShield Executive OS. Current prototype build is V60.3.6. Do not call the public build CyberShield OS v8 unless the repo, README, loader, Settings/admin metadata, and public UX intentionally move to that version scheme.
v60_3_6_correction_rule: Do not create a new TrustMap overlay. Do not replace the radar / constellation environment. Do not move Layer 1 assets out of their current positions. Only improve the visual representation inside each existing Layer 1 asset bubble.

public_trustmap_language.title: TrustMap
public_trustmap_language.subtitle: Decision Trust Universe
public_trustmap_language.rule: Do not expose builder language such as registry-driven, renders from registry, versioned render source, or V55.3 registry in executive-facing TrustMap UI.

trustmap_visual_doctrine: The radar and constellation background is the environment. The existing Layer 1 bubbles are the interface. The active decision is the gravity center.
score_rule: Every visible score must be clickable and must explain or route to the model, evidence, assumptions, missing inputs, confidence, reliance risk, and decision rationale behind that score.
trust_distribution_rule: Operational Trust Score is a current decision posture score. Trust Level Distribution is a count or percentage distribution across Layer 1 trust assets. They should not show the same number unless intentionally representing the same metric.
information_architecture_rule: Evidence tab owns the full Manual Evidence Workbench. Runtime tab owns compact evidence requirements for action. Proof Pack tab owns proof-oriented evidence trace and defensibility context. Settings owns build/version/governance/prototype metadata.
controlling_doctrine: No score without a model. No model without evidence. No evidence without traceability. No action without consequence.
model_maturity_rule: CyberShield scoring models are Expert-Derived V1 Trust Scoring Models, not Statistically Validated Trust Models. Default weights are calibratable and not authoritative until validated through test scenarios, expert review, red-team attempts, and real-world use cases.
v60_3_6_changes: removes the V60.3.5 overlay import from src/ui/v52-7-operational-layer.js
preserves the existing radar / constellation TrustMap
preserves the existing three-ring spatial model
preserves existing Layer 1 asset positions and click behavior
replaces flat inner icons inside existing Layer 1 asset bubbles with more dimensional holographic object artwork
renders Cloud & Infrastructure as a dimensional cloud with infrastructure glow beneath it
renders CMMC & Compliance as a Pentagon-inspired dimensional building form inside the existing bubble
renders identity, data, AI, vendor, and endpoint assets as richer volumetric objects
removes the mistaken floating TrustMap overlay layer from the operational path
keeps public TrustMap language as TrustMap and Decision Trust Universe
preserves V60.3.4 explicit actionability and modal disclosure architecture

trustmap_asset_rendering_correction.rule: Preserve existing TrustMap radar, constellation, rings, object positions, and interactions. Replace only the artwork inside existing Layer 1 asset bubbles.
trustmap_asset_rendering_correction.boundary: Static advisory visual correction only. No live scoring, evidence retrieval, workflow automation, ticketing, notifications, backend persistence, enforcement, statistical validation, certification, compliance validation, or live integrations.

recommended_next_build.label: V60.3.7 TrustMap Asset Artwork Tuning
goals: confirm existing TrustMap interactions remain intact
preserve no-new-top-level-tabs and Settings-only governance metadata

implemented_release_train.v56: Trust Model Registry and Score Explanation Layer
implemented_release_train.v59.5: Internet Trust QA and Copy Guardrail Pass
implemented_release_train.v60.3.3: First-Layer Decision Brief and TrustMap Snapshot

prototype_boundary: The current GitHub Pages build is a lightweight static advisory prototype. It is not connected to live enterprise telemetry, SIEM, EDR, IAM, Microsoft 365, cloud control planes, GRC tooling, production agent enforcement, Google Sheets, CRM sync, ticketing, notifications, marketplace systems, ad platforms, domain-intelligence systems, identity verification systems, CMMC certification systems, healthcare compliance validation systems, banking/payment systems, live monitoring, live URL ingestion, live internet retrieval, live evidence retrieval, live scoring, live claim extraction, internet claim verification, report delivery, ticketing, workflow automation, notifications, backend persistence, statistical validation, or takedown automation.
known_limitations: Dimensional artwork is CSS/SVG-based and may require visual tuning
Models are expert-derived V1 and not statistically validated
No live scoring, evidence retrieval, ticketing, notifications, or workflow automation
GitHub Pages browser QA required after deploy
